title: Any Eva releases in widescreen
wikitext:
[]: Any Eva releases in widescreen?             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Sun Apr 11, 2010 7:05 pm This is a dumb question , but are there any releases of the Eva series that are widescreen? I have the platinum adv box set which is 4:3

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Apr 11, 2010 7:17 pm Nope. The show was made in 4:3, so there's no extra information off to the sides that you're not seeing. (Or, if there is in the actual cels, it's incomplete because you weren't supposed to see it.) If you want Eva in widescreen, you could put pieces of black construction paper over the top and bottom of your TV, but it'd sort of defeat the point.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sun Apr 11, 2010 7:18 pm esselfortium wrote: If you want Eva in widescreen, you could put pieces of black construction paper over the top and bottom of your TV, but it'd sort of defeat the point.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Apr 11, 2010 7:20 pm esselfortium wrote: If you want Eva in widescreen, you could put pieces of black construction paper over the top and bottom of your TV, but it'd sort of defeat the point.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Apr 11, 2010 7:32 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: Or a digital zoom or stretch the aspect ration if the DVD or television being used has that feature. Stretched is really ugly though.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Apr 11, 2010 7:33 pm It's a terrible, terrible habit of mine.

[]:             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Sun Apr 11, 2010 8:17 pm Thanks for the info guys, and happy to join to the forums and talk /research Eva. I was at this sites panel at Tekkoshocon, and it was amazing, who is the admin here or creator that was at Tekko?

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Sun Apr 11, 2010 8:29 pm ProfessorKaos64 wrote: I was at this sites panel at Tekkoshocon, and it was amazing, who is the admin here or creator that was at Tekko?

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Sun Apr 11, 2010 8:44 pm Baz wrote: See the following thread, for you shall meet our fearless leader there.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Tue Apr 13, 2010 2:26 pm I see. Not wanting to see it as it was originally intended, eh? Let me say one thing: RESPECT THE PRESENTATION! If you crop the image, fine, but you will regret it!

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Tue Apr 13, 2010 10:26 pm This is why they should re-do NGE cel by cel just like dragonball kai. There is a damand for it and good knows it needs it. Just my opinion. :/ ...we get blu-ray quality updated nge, gainax gets mo monay! Why isn't this happening!? Is this happening? This should happen.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Apr 14, 2010 12:02 am skikes wrote: This is why they should re-do NGE cel by cel just like dragonball kai.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Wed Apr 14, 2010 7:29 am Sachi wrote: Hello Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Wed Apr 14, 2010 11:03 am skikes wrote: Have you seen rebuild? 0_o ...or dragonball kai for that matter.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Apr 14, 2010 11:36 am tomrule123 wrote: Rebuild of EVA is reanimating the scenes from scratch and using the lastest CGI technology to present the visual effects in the best way possible.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Wed Apr 14, 2010 11:53 am tomrule123 wrote: I have seen both. Rebuild of EVA is reanimating the scenes from scratch and using the lastest CGI technology to present the visual effects in the best way possible.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Apr 14, 2010 12:24 pm skikes wrote: Rebuild is an entirely new series of movies. While yes, some scenes are similar to the original if not down right copies, the plot and characters are almost completely different.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Wed Apr 14, 2010 12:48 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Well, slightly different. Then again, I haven't seen 2.0 yet. 1.11 didn't have the characters stray too far from the original.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Wed Apr 14, 2010 10:53 pm skikes wrote: Every cell has been redrawn

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Thu Apr 15, 2010 12:17 pm Up to todays standards, huh? Last episodes of NGE actually look rather sharp, especially any scene that was added in the DC (or NPC, whatever).
